SUBJECTIVE:
Milo, 4y MN Labrador. Diarrhoea since yesterday morning, first noted about 6am yesterday, 6-7 episodes yesterday and 3 this morning. Stool watery with mucus, tiny red streak once last night. One vomit yesterday afternoon, yellow/foamy, none overnight.

OBJECTIVE:
Bright, responsive, friendly, slightly quieter than typical. Wt 28.4 kg. T 38.7C, HR 108, RR 28. MM pink/moist. CRT<2. Mild cranial abdo discomfort on deep palpation, no obvious mass, no severe guarding.

ASSESSMENT:
Acute gastroenteritis, likely dietary indiscretion.

PLAN:
Maropitant 1 mg/kg SC in clinic. Pro-Kolin 5 ml PO q8h x3 days. Bland diet small frequent meals x3 days, then transition to normal food over 2-3 days if stools improve. Encourage water. Avoid high-fat treats and no new foods this week. Short lead walks only today.
